steps.title: Identify the self-interested motive
steps.content:
The key signal is “self-interested” — climbers' exaggerated claims came from personal motivation.

The passage establishes that edelweiss aren't notably harder to reach than other Swiss mountain flowers, so why would climbers falsely claim they grew only in steep, dangerous terrain?

The answer must explain the personal benefit.

steps.title: Match the personal benefit
steps.content: The correct answer explains the self-interested motive directly: by claiming edelweiss were in dangerous places, climbers who reached them could present themselves as brave and strong. The other choices don't explain this personal gain. That is the correct answer.
